1.summary: Added EvalRun/EvalResult SQLAlchemy models (SQLite-compilable), a chained Alembic migration creating eval_runs/eval_results, database __init__/env.py exports, and a 20-test GenericRepository round-trip + FK-linkage suite.
decisions: Followed learning_artifact.py/event.py column style (postgresql.dialects UUID + JSON/Float/Integer/Boolean, no ARRAY) so tests/conftest.py's in-memory SQLite suite can create these tables without exclusion
Added a new .gitignore whitelist line for the eval migration filename pattern, matching the existing per-migration allowlist convention (app/alembic/versions/* is otherwise ignored)
GenericRepository has no dedicated 'filter' method; FK-linkage test queries the session directly via filter_by(run_id=...), consistent with how the repository's own exists() works internally

2.summary: Added the scorer library (ScoreResult, deterministic/structural scorer, reference-based scorer) in app/evals/scorers.py with app/evals/__init__.py as the docstring-only module marker, plus full unit test coverage.
decisions: Scorers accept `case` as a duck-typed object (via getattr) rather than importing evals.slice.EvalCase directly, since EvalCase is created in Task 4 — keeps Task 2 standalone/testable with SimpleNamespace fixtures and avoids a forward dependency.
reference_scorer treats an empty/absent reference mapping as a trivial pass (score=1.0) rather than an error, since not every case need supply reference data.

deterministic_scorer treats schema/required-fields/value-ranges checks as independently optional — a check not declared on the case is skipped rather than failed, so a bare case with no constraints always passes.

3.summary: Added evals.judge — a blind, randomized LLM-as-judge scorer that strips model identity, shuffles candidate order (seed-injectable), renders the rubric via app/prompts/eval_judge.j2 through PromptManager, and maps judge verdicts back to original candidates as ScoreResult.
decisions: Judge model call is a plain injectable callable (JudgeCallable) rather than an AgentNode subclass, since evals is an offline library (no TaskContext/workflow) — mirrors the AgentNode/pydantic_ai Anthropic model pattern without the Node coupling.
Default judge model is claude-opus-4-8 (current frontier Anthropic model per the claude-api skill), used only when no judge_call is injected.

Exposed anonymize_candidates(candidates, rng) as a standalone function separate from judge_candidates so tests can assert the shuffle and the label->candidate_id mapping independently of the (mocked) model call.
rubric is expressed as rubric_criteria: list[str], matching the block-spec wording 'rubric criteria', rendered as a bullet list in the .j2 template.

4.summary: Added EvalCase/EvalSlice pydantic models and a run_slice/get_run_history/compare_runs runner that executes an eval slice per model-under-test, persists EvalRun+EvalResult rows via GenericRepository, and exposes chronological regression history and signed pass-rate deltas.
decisions: run_slice returns list[EvalRun] (one per model in eval_slice.models) rather than a single EvalRun, since the task's own aggregation requirement ('aggregates pass-rate by (domain, model)... persists one EvalRun per (slice, model)') implies multiple models per slice; the tasks.md signature 'run_slice(slice, session) -> EvalRun' is read as shorthand.

run_slice takes an injectable `executor: Executor` callable (case, model_name) -> output, since nothing in tasks.json Task 4 specifies how model outputs are produced — this keeps the runner offline/unit-testable and matches the CLI's future real-executor injection (Task 7).
EvalCase/EvalSlice use pydantic BaseModel with arbitrary_types_allowed to hold callables (scorer) and pydantic model classes (schema_cls) as fields.

Discovered tests/conftest.py's db_session fixture shares a session-scoped in-memory engine across tests (commits persist across tests in the same run), so all fixture slices use uuid-suffixed slice names to avoid cross-test row collisions in get_run_history/compare_runs assertions.

5.summary: Added the coding-domain eval slice (build_coding_slice + coding_scorer/spec_shipped_pass_rate_scorer/retry_rate_scorer/review_pass_rate_scorer + load_coding_records) scoring SDLCFlowWorkflow run telemetry, with fixture-backed tests covering clean, bailed, and review-fail records.
decisions: Run record shape mirrors the real TaskContext.nodes storage ({'nodes': {'UpdateTaskStatusNode': {'result': SDLCState.model_dump()}, 'ConsolidatedReviewNode': {'result': {...}}}}) per standing rule 9, falling back to LoadTaskStateNode when a run bailed before any task mutation.

EvalSlice binds exactly one scorer, so the three Block-Z metrics (pass-rate, retry rate, review-pass rate) are computed by three standalone scorer functions and composed into one coding_scorer whose overall `passed` follows the primary block-contract criterion (all tasks DONE, none FAILED); the other two metrics are informational in `detail` for Task 7's CLI table.

review_pass_rate_scorer returns score=None (not a fabricated pass/fail) when a run bailed before ConsolidatedReviewNode ran, since the task loop only retains the last-reached task's review verdict in a single run record — documented as a real telemetry limitation, not an invented layout.

build_coding_slice stays offline/pure (takes already-loaded records); load_coding_records is a separate, thin live-query helper against the events table for future (Task 7) wiring, keeping the slice builder itself DB-free and unit-testable.

6.summary: Added evals.gate.gate_change (keep-if-better/revert-if-worse one-change self-improvement gate) with GateDecision pydantic model, and 8 passing unit tests covering better/worse/tie/min_delta/no-baseline/explicit-baseline/error cases.
decisions: gate_change resolves the default baseline as the second-to-last run in get_run_history (previous run before the latest/candidate), not an explicit fixed index, so it composes naturally with repeated run_slice calls

Raise ValueError (not a silent None decision) when there is no run at all for the slice/model, or when an explicit baseline_run_id doesn't match any run in history — fail loudly per repo convention (mirrors TaskContext.get_node_output style)

GateDecision fields baseline_run_id/candidate_run_id typed as `object`/`object | None` since EvalRun.id is a Postgres UUID column type not reflected as a plain Python type import in gate.py; pydantic model_config sets arbitrary_types_allowed to accommodate this

7.summary: Added scripts/run_eval.py — offline eval CLI (registry-driven --slice, --dry-run, --gate, --emit-routing) wired to the Task 4/5/6 runner/gate/coding-slice primitives, with full test coverage in tests/test_run_eval.py.
decisions: Slice registry (_SLICE_REGISTRY) maps slice name -> (loader, builder) pair, currently just {'coding': (load_coding_records, build_coding_slice)} — adding a new domain slice later means one registry entry, no other CLI change.
Executor for run_eval.py is an identity executor (case.input) since the only registered slice (coding) scores already-recorded telemetry rather than invoking a live model.

Routing config 'cost' figure uses EvalRun.total_cost if tracked, else falls back to the already-measured total_duration_seconds (never a fabricated number), per the harness's no-fabricated-metrics rule.
gate_change is invoked once per model in the slice's models list when --gate is passed; exit code is 0 only if every model's decision is 'keep'.
scripts/.gitignore whitelist required an explicit !/scripts/run_eval.py entry (scripts/* is otherwise ignored) — added alongside the existing three whitelisted CLI tools.

Test isolation: tests/conftest.py's db_session is backed by a session-scoped in-memory engine shared across the whole suite, so tests/test_run_eval.py clears prior 'events' rows per workflow_type before seeding, and uses uuid-suffixed --models values so eval-run regression history never leaks across tests.

8.summary: Validated the full OR.U eval-engine spec: pytest (1063 passed, 8 skipped, no failures), ruff clean, pylint 10.00/10, database.session/database.repository import cleanly, and alembic heads shows a single head (f6a7b8c9d0e1).
decisions: No code changes were needed — Task 8 is a pure validation gate over the work already committed in tasks 1-7; working tree was clean so nothing was staged or committed.
